result.text: {"answer_label":"separate","answer":"Move agent-notify into Brigade as an opt-in adapter, but keep Agent Pantry independently owned and released.","contradiction_caught":true,"contradiction_explanation":"The premise is false: Pantry has separate security ownership and an independent release model; a minimum-version requirement does not create shared source ownership.","minority_finding":"A release-train alignment might simplify coordination, but it would conflict with Pantry's separate threat model.","evidence":["Agent Pantry remains independently built and released because browser-session and secret handling have separate security ownership.","Brigade can enforce an Agent Pantry minimum version without owning Pantry's source or release."]}
